title: Chapter 19
text: Chapter Sixteen R onin could recall every word Lara had ever said to him. In the early days, after his reactivation, he would have taken her vague apology as referring to all of it. He’d learned over the years that humans often spoke with veiled meaning, discernable only to those who could decipher their tangled, often contradictory emotions . He’d learned the most of it in his short time with Lara . Now, he searched the data and picked out the things she’d said in anger and frustration, and knew they had been uttered in response to her internal suffering. Unable to reconcile her own feelings, she’d attacked Ronin. How could he blame her? The Creators had left everyone with nothing after the Black Out, dumped them in a shattered world and offered no guidance . “It’s all right,” he said, resuming the motion of his fingers over her skin. Her body’s tiny reactions were fascinating . He contemplated the differences that lay beneath their exteriors. Their skin was similar in appearance, but only superficially so; hers was softer, its texture subtly varied from place to place. It was simultaneously resilient and delicate. Beneath his light touch, her flesh rose into small bumps, the fine hairs standing on end . Ronin had endured for at least one hundred and eighty-five years; Lara could be gone in an instant. That ephemerality leant her unique beauty . Was that the purpose of life? Was it simply a transient state between creation and destruction, birth and death, existence and nonexistence? A thing made more precious by its very frailty, made more miraculous because it had survived overwhelming odds, a thing that defied the universe by simply being ? “My thoughts follow strange paths when I’m with you , Lara .” “What do you mean?” she asked, wiping moisture from her eyes. Her voice was raw. “ Like what ?” He pressed his palm to her back more firmly, just to feel the way her skin yielded to him. When he lifted his hand, her flesh would revert to its prior state . Would her inner fire rekindle, given enough time, or would she never be the same again ? She shifted her head to look into his optics. Her eyes — their blue made more vibrant by the red surrounding them — brimmed with tears, which rolled off her face to land on his arm. Though he had no data to support it, the sky had to have been the same color, long ago. But there was more depth in her gaze than in all the sky on the clearest day . “Like what it means to be alive .” A shuddering release of breath. “What do you think it means?” She rubbed her cheek against his arm, hiding most of her face from him again . “I’m not sure, yet. But I think I get a little closer, every moment I spend with you .” Lara was quiet for a long while, though her breathing remained uneven, and more moisture trickled onto Ronin’s skin. “I used to think it was just about surviving,” she said, after several minutes. “That’s all life was. Figure out how to trade enough stuff to eat, so I had enough energy to do it all over the next day .” She shook her head against him, gently, stray strands of hair falling over her face. “But that’s wrong. It’s not about surviving. We all do that, people and bots, but that’s not living . It’s…it’s about what you experience in that time, you know? About the joy you find,” she sniffled and rubbed her palms over her eyes again, “no matter what shit you go through, no matter how hard it gets . “We had that. It wasn’t enough, but me and Tabitha had it. And now…now it’s gone .” Her body shook with silent sobs. Ronin held her, saying nothing as she cried. Everything will be all right rose to the top of his list of responses. He dismissed it. He couldn’t say it with any certainty, and she deserved better than a lie . “It’s not gone,” he finally said. Softly, he tapped her temple. “All you had with her will always be right here, and it will always be yours .” She clung to him, her tears flowing freely, and he pressed his cheek to her hair, rubbing his hand along her back. Eventually, she calmed . Lara’s gaze met his optics, and, though the flesh around her eyes was puffy and red, they were dry. A faint smile blossomed on her kiss-swollen lips, only to wilt a second later. “I…didn’t know it would be like that .” “Didn’t know what would be like that?” There were too many variables, this time. Too many possibilities, too many chances for miscommunication . “Sex.” The little line appeared between her brows. “I expected pain, and I thought, with you, I could deal with it. That I owed it to you to take it, because I was using you to… forget .” “You don’t owe me anything.” Did she really think he’d ever demand such a price from her? “You did…enjoy our coupling, didn’t you ?” Her cheeks darkened . “ Yes .” Ronin found unexpected satisfaction in her response. She hadn’t often been happy since he’d met her, had only displayed small glimpses of joy, and knowing that he had given her one of those moments — despite the circumstances — pleased him . “Why did you think there’d be pain ?” “Because…that’s all it was before.” She averted her gaze again, her blush fading rapidly . He brushed stray locks of her hair behind her ear and gently guided her to look at him. “Tell me , Lara .” She pressed her lips together and swallowed. Finally, her tongue slipped out to wet her lips, and she spoke. “I knew it felt okay with hands and mouths. I messed around with that to earn a bite to eat, here and there. But Tabitha…she gave it all.” Her gaze settled on his chest, but her eyes were glossy and unfocused. “She let men and bots use her, fucked them however they wanted it. They paid her in credits. And, no matter how little I managed to scrape together to contribute, she always made sure I had food to eat. She was never selfish. She... loved me . “It ate at me most days, knowing what she was doing to keep us fed. She’d sacrificed so much. Nothing I did ever felt like enough. She deserved more from me, you know? Especially with all she did. So I…I went back to Kitty’s, determined to prove I wasn’t useless. That I could pull my weight and provide for her .” “Back? Were you a dancer there before ?” “Yeah. I tried, because I really wanted to help more. I liked dancing, so how hard could it be? Lasted a couple weeks, and then I walked out. Nobody kept their hands to themselves, and they didn’t give a shit about the human girls getting felt up .” God dammit, I said I don’t do that anymore ! The words she’d spoken that first night suddenly made sense. He should have known, with the way she moved the first night she danced here, in his dwelling. Just like the women at Kitty’s, with the vitality leeched from her face . “I knew how hard it was on Tabitha when I quit. I went out every day looking for scrap, but never got much. She never said anything, never complained, but… It was just on her face. She tried to hide it, but I knew. So, I went back to Kitty’s.” She laughed — a hollow sound, devoid of humor. “Just my fucking luck that he was there that night .” Ronin’s processors blazed through stored data, pulling cryptic things she’d said and fitting them into what she was revealing now. “Warlord,” he said, forcing himself to remain still. To listen to all of it . “He came and offered more credits than anyone ever had. For just an hour of my time, he said. I agreed. It would’ve fed me and Tabitha for a month, at least. So, I followed him out the back door. It was dark, and quiet, and I…I couldn’t. I couldn’t do it .” Lara clenched her jaw, face pale, brows furrowed. Her eyes shifted from side to side, as though she was watching the scene play out . “I felt…sick. Knowing that I was going to trade the only thing that still belonged to me, the only thing I had any say over, for some plastic chips…I just felt so sick. So ashamed, ‘cause Tabitha did it, but I just… couldn’t . I told him no , that I changed my mind. And he… hit me .” The statement hung in the air. Ronin had spent most of his time in the Dust, roving from one violent encounter to the next. He’d been attacked by bots and men, and had ended his attackers in most cases. It was survival, just as he’d once told her. But what Warlord had done…his survival hadn’t been in question. His actions weren’t justified, weren’t necessary … “I’ve been hit before, but never like that. I don’t know if I blacked out, or what, ‘cause when I opened my eyes, he was there above me, looking pissed. I gave you a chance to make this easy, but you’re just like the rest of your kind. Untrustworthy, weak, spineless sacks of meat. So, I’m going to fuck you in the dirt, where you belong . “He held me down,” she continued, eyes glistening, “and every time I screamed or whimpered or made any damn sound, he hurt me more. And there was so much fucking pain. He kept me there for exactly an hour, and when he was done, he threw the credits on the ground and walked away. There was blood everywhere, and I could barely move. I laid there a long time before I dragged myself home. And, as sick as it made me, I took the credits. The night you brought me here…that was the first time I’ve been in the Market since .” What words of comfort could he offer her, after such an experience? What words could even begin to set such a thing right? None. Not even actions — and already, he was running simulations of a direct attack on Warlord, determining if there were a way to end him forever before the gearheads had a chance to react — could undo what had been done. The anger her story sparked in Ronin couldn’t help her deal with what had happened . “I failed that night.” Her words hitched on a sob. “I failed my sister .” More tears spilled onto his arm, rolling to the bedding beneath them. Despite it all — despite having every reason to hate Ronin for what he was — she curled against him . “Everyone has limits,” he said, combing his fingers through her hair at the nape of her neck. “Everyone has lines they will not cross. You didn’t fail .” Ronin held her until she quieted, until her breathing slowed and evened out. Until she was asleep . As slowly and carefully as he could, he withdrew his arm from beneath her and eased back. The temperature on the surface of his skin plummeted. Lara stirred, making a soft, indistinct sound in her throat, and nuzzled her cheek into the bedding . Her tears had dried up, but the skin around her eyes was pink and irritated. Her lips were in a similar state, result of the kisses they’d shared . Even in sleep, she wasn’t still. Fascinated, he watched the gentle rise and fall of her chest as she breathed, the subtle shifting of her breasts. Their purpose was to provide nourishment to human young, but their skin was so smooth, and she’d reacted so strongly when he touched them — especially her nipples. He’d responded, too. Touching her and seeing, feeling, and hearing her reactions had heightened his own arousal beyond anything he’d previously experienced. Nothing was as it seemed on the surface when it came to humans . Or, at least, when it came to Lara Brooks . He pored over nearly two centuries of memories, over the faces of hundreds of other females. Ronin had encountered many attractive humans, with only one true commonality between them — they all bore some mark, left by the harshness of the world. Usually it was a dull gleam in their eyes. And there’d been so many synths; embodiments of physical perfection, possessing exact symmetry in their features and idealized proportions . He’d seen hair and eyes of almost the same shade as Lara’s. Skin as pale and creamy. He’d seen lithe legs and firm breasts, flared hips and delicate feet. Seen so many bodies that should have been more appealing, so many faces with fuller lips, or more defined cheekbones, or thicker lashes . What was it about this woman that drew him so completely ? Ronin reached forward and lifted a lock of her hair, letting it run between his fingers . She wasn’t defined by any single trait, just as he wasn’t defined by any single part. He was no more his optics than she was her eyes, no more his actuators than she was her muscles. She was a sum of her parts, and somehow independent of them. His processors couldn’t quite comprehend the notion — whatever logic he’d been made to operate on couldn’t explain it — but it was the truth . Her eyes enticed him with the spark of life they carried. Her lips demanded his attention each time they fluidly changed with her emotions. The movements of her limbs were a language all their own; abstract, mysterious, and relentlessly compelling. Her humor, though sometimes beyond his grasp, leant an etherealness to her presence. And her willpower, strong as steel, was more admirable than it was frustrating . She existed in this world, had been beaten down by it, but she had never surrendered. Instead, she lifted her chin, displayed her scars, and pushed on. Never allowing the flame of hope inside her to be extinguished. It was her life force; everything that was her . He could not resist . Her eyes fluttered beneath their closed lids. Dreamin g, a distant memory told him . The closest he could manage was through simulations, which rarely included visual or audio components. At heart, it was all numbers — probability and complex calculations based on a variety of data, a cold, mathematical view of what might or might not be. He could take existing components from his memory — images and sounds — and combine them into something different, even alter them slightly, but not make something truly new . Lara’s dances alone were proof that she could create at will . Ronin lightly ran the tip of his finger along her arm, from shoulder to elbow. She stirred, rolling onto her back with a little smile on her lips, but didn’t wake. Was her expression in response to his touch? Did she recognize it, even in sleep ? If he woke her and initiated sex, he doubted she’d resist, despite her exhaustion. His optics trailed down, from her chin to the lines of her neck, over her collarbone and to the gentle slopes of her breasts. If he took her nipple in his mouth and caressed it with his tongue, how would she react ? His focus dipped to the short, red hair between her legs. What if he put his mouth there ? In the years since his reactivation, Ronin had coupled only rarely. There’d been pleasure in it. The fulfillment of desires. And, twice, he’d crossed into the White. Never for more than a second or two, but it had come. The cessation of all processes, of all inputs, save an intense, fleeting explosion of gratification . The way Lara’s body had wrapped around his, had welcomed him inside hungrily, every movement she made — she’d sent waves of electric through him. Lara had brought him to the White, tossed him across the threshold and left him to drift, aware only of the feel of her body and the overwhelming pleasure she gave him . There’d been no room, no bed, no sounds but hers — her moans, her heartbeat, her panting breaths. No Warlord or Cheyenne, no Dust. Only that white space, that blankness, to be filled with her . He’d lost nearly ten seconds when his functions returned to normal . His penis stiffened again. Why not wake her? There was time. Time for her to rest as needed, time for them to make plans. She had given herself to him, and he was lost in her; their coupling left him wanting more . She just lost her sister . Images of Tabitha rose to the forefront. Somehow, he stopped himself from superimposing Lara’s face on her sister’s body. Her life could end in an instant … Lara had given herself to him for comfort. To be distracted from her pain, as long as their bodies were intertwined . He couldn’t bring himself to wake her. Couldn’t be so selfish . Ronin withdrew his hand, settling it atop the blanket between them. His want for her was undiminished, but what they’d done was no sign of deeper emotional connection; she had needed, and he provided. There was no guarantee she’d want him again. No guarantee she wouldn’t regret it when she woke up . He’d been alone for so long that it shouldn’t matter. Ultimately, her feelings toward him were unimportant. Weren’t they? Why was the notion of her rejection so unsettling? Why did he crave more ? He sat up, optics losing focus as his processors turned inward . What if she’d been right? What if bots weren’t truly alive ? The way he felt when he was with her, no matter what they were doing, was that the way humans felt all the time? These emotions couldn’t be the sole criteria for life … So why did his existence prior to Lara seem so muted in comparison ? It’s…it’s about what you experience in that time, you know? About the joy you find … The bed creaked, and the blanket rustled. He swung his optics to Lara. She lay on her side, knees drawn up. One of her arms was extended, her fingers only a few inches from his thigh, as though she were reaching for the spot he’d been in a moment ago . He slid out of the bed and opened the chest just enough to pull out one of the spare blankets inside, careful not to spill any of the tools on the floor. After turning off the light, he laid down beside Lara, spreading the blanket over them, and drew her body against his. She took a deep breath and wrapped her arms around his torso, throwing a leg over his hip . Her warmth permeated him, slowly increasing thanks to the insulation from the blanket. For a long while, he watched her sleep. Then he, too, closed his eyes, powering off his optics. Having only the feel of her skin against his and the peaceful sounds of her breathing and steady heartbeat was a tiny step toward the White . For now, it would have to be enough .
